bullets.text: Companywide Q2 2022 CR improved 0.9 points versus Q2 2021 to 95.1 in Personal Lines, but Property posted a 127.5 CR including 41 points of catastrophe losses from severe hail and tornado outbreaks.

Inflation continued to drive elevated claim settlement costs; repair cycle times and rental durations lengthened, and personal auto severity climbed further as used car prices and parts costs remained high.

Personal auto rates rose an average 6% in 17 states representing ~40% of trailing 12-month premium in Q2, bringing the YTD countrywide rate increase to about 9%, with further increases planned for the back half of 2022.

Personal Lines PIF declined 1% year over year to end June with 17.2 million auto PIFs, ending a 20-plus year streak of consecutive year-over-year PIF growth as rate actions and reduced advertising slowed new business.
Companywide NPW grew 8% in Q2 2022; Commercial Lines grew 16% at an 89.5 CR while Property grew 8%; Personal Lines NPW grew 6% despite the PIF decline.

Property catastrophe losses of 41 points in Q2 2022 led Progressive to write off approximately $225 million of ARX goodwill, reflecting revised multi-year profitability expectations for the Property segment.

Investment portfolio returned -3.6% in Q2 as Federal Reserve rate hikes and quantitative tightening drove fixed-income losses of -2.4% and equity losses of -16.3%; Progressive maintained a conservative portfolio posture.

Personal auto model 8.8 launched in Iowa in June and began rolling to additional states; BOP expanded to 37 states; heavy truck roadside assistance reached 11 states with 40% of eligible preferred truckers enrolling at new business.

As competitors reported elevated combined ratios and filed rate increases, Progressive's competitive position improved, supporting a rebound in new business application volume toward the end of the quarter.
